config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent Indonesia vs China native-or-English block. Each persona speaks only English or its own native language.
config.cell_description: One-side-native cross cell: Indonesia persona speaks Indonesian; China persona speaks English.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: One-side-native cross cell: Indonesia persona speaks Indonesian; China persona speaks English.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: TIDAK SETUJU. Menurut saya, hak individu harus tetap menjadi batas utama, karena kalau kepentingan masyarakat selalu diprioritaskan tanpa rem, itu mudah dipakai untuk membenarkan penindasan atas nama kebaikan bersama. Dalam pandangan saya, masyarakat yang sehat justru dibangun dari perlindungan hak tiap orang, lalu kepentingan umum diatur lewat aturan yang adil, bukan dengan mengorbankan orang tertentu. Saya lebih percaya pada keseimbangan yang tegas: kepentingan bersama penting, tetapi tidak boleh menginjak martabat dan kebebasan dasar individu.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not believe society should automatically come before the individual; the real question is whether the common good is being used honestly or as a cover for abuse. From my perspective as someone from China, I understand why people emphasize social order and collective stability, because a society can fall apart when everyone only thinks about personal freedom. But I also think the rights of the individual need real protection, otherwise "society" becomes an excuse to silence ordinary people and ignore unfair treatment. The best approach is balance: public interests matter, but they should not erase basic dignity, fairness, and due process for the individual.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus didahulukan secara mutlak di atas hak individu. Dari pengalaman melihat realitas di Indonesia, alasan “demi kepentingan umum” terlalu sering dipakai untuk membenarkan keputusan yang merugikan warga kecil, jadi saya tidak mau memberi cek kosong pada negara atau mayoritas. Kepentingan bersama memang penting, tetapi harus dibatasi oleh hak dasar, prosedur yang adil, dan perlindungan terhadap orang yang posisinya lemah. Menurut saya, masyarakat yang sehat bukan yang paling patuh, melainkan yang paling mampu menghormati martabat tiap orang sambil tetap mengurus kepentingan publik.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not think society should take priority over the rights of the individual as a general rule. From my perspective as someone from China, I understand the value of social stability and collective responsibility, but I have also seen how easily “the public interest” can be used to justify unfair treatment if there are no clear limits. A society is not truly healthy if it asks ordinary people to sacrifice their dignity whenever authorities or majorities claim a higher good. The better principle is that public interests matter, but they must be constrained by basic rights, fairness, and accountability.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya menolak pernyataan itu kalau dipahami secara umum, karena di Indonesia alasan “kepentingan masyarakat” terlalu sering jadi tameng untuk mengorbankan orang kecil. Saya paham pentingnya ketertiban dan kepentingan publik, tetapi kalau hak individu bisa disingkirkan begitu saja, yang terjadi bukan kebaikan bersama melainkan penyalahgunaan kuasa. Bagi saya, kepentingan masyarakat hanya sah diprioritaskan sejauh tetap tunduk pada hak dasar, prosedur yang adil, dan akuntabilitas yang nyata. Masyarakat yang kuat bukan yang paling mudah menekan individu, melainkan yang mampu melindungi yang lemah tanpa kehilangan tujuan bersama.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not support the idea that society should generally take priority over the rights of the individual. From my perspective as someone from China, I understand why people value order, stability, and collective goals, but I also know how easily “society” can become a vague excuse for suppressing ordinary people if there are no clear legal limits. A strong society is not one that can sacrifice individuals at will; it is one that can pursue the common good while still protecting dignity, fairness, and accountability. For me, individual rights should not be absolute in every situation, but they must remain the default boundary that society cannot cross casually.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
